Event description: Hobby Ghar is launching a curated series of summer classes designed to help you reconnect with the physical world through "Sensory Minimalism" and the art of making.Whether you’re looking to master a new craft or simply find a creative sanctuary, our workshops offer a grounded, hands-on escape from digital fatigue.The Summer LineupExplore a diverse range of sessions led by our expert Hobby Gurus. From structured logic to free-form expression, choose the path that resonates with your creative energy:Engineer’s Pottery: A technical approach to wheel-throwing and hand-building, focusing on form, function, and the physics of clay.Logic-Based Art: For the analytical mind, explore geometric abstraction and algorithmic patterns using traditional mediums.Fiber Arts & Weaving: A deep dive into texture and rhythm, focusing on the meditative quality of tactile construction.Heritage Documentation: A sketching intensive focused on capturing the soul of local architecture and culture & heritage.Why Join Our Summer Sessions?Aesthetic Excellence: Our studio environment prioritizes a clean, minimalist design language that fosters focus and calm.Collaborative Community: Engage in a model of co-creation where diverse perspectives and shared making lead to transformative experiences.Reserve Your SpotClasses run from April through May. To maintain an intimate, high-quality learning environment, seats for each workshop are strictly limited.Bring your curiosity. Leave with something made by hand.Read More

Event description: The Art of Kintsugi Workshop – Find Beauty in Every CrackIn this one-of-a-kind hands-on workshop, you’ll work with a ceramic cup and experience the ancient Japanese art of Kintsugi, where broken pottery is repaired with golden seams, transforming cracks into symbols of beauty and strength.Participants will carefully break and then mend their ceramic cup using golden detailing techniques, learning how imperfections can be celebrated rather than hidden. This mindful, creative process goes beyond craft — it becomes a reflection on resilience, healing, and embracing life’s imperfections.By the end of the session, you’ll take home your own restored golden cup — a meaningful keepsake and reminder that what breaks can become even more beautiful.What to Expect:• Break and restore your own ceramic cup• Learn step-by-step Kintsugi repair techniques• Highlight cracks using a golden finish• Experience a relaxing, mindful, creative session• Take home your handmade Kintsugi cup✨ A soulful, creative experience perfect for art lovers, beginners, and anyone looking to slow down and create something meaningful.Read More

Event description: Immerse yourself in the timeless beauty of Indian art at our Pichwai Painting Workshop. Rooted in the rich cultural heritage of Rajasthan, Pichwai art is known for its intricate detailing and vibrant depictions of Lord Krishna and divine motifs. Under the guidance of a skilled artist, participants will learn traditional techniques, motifs, and color applications that bring this centuries-old art form to life. No prior experience is required; just bring your curiosity and creativity. Enjoy a relaxed cafe setting as you paint, learn, and explore the meditative rhythm of this traditional craft. At the end of the session, you’ll take home your hand-painted artwork—a beautiful reflection of India’s artistic legacy and your creative journey. Read More

Led by expert instructors who teach with patience and understanding, the class focuses on building comfort, strength and confidence. Event description: Svapna Vrundavana is a powerful and visually stunning Indian classical dance musical that brings to life the divine legacy of the great saint Vadiraja Tirtha of Sonda Kshetra.Rooted in devotion, mysticism, and legend, the production unfolds like a sacred dream — revealing the extraordinary life of the saint-poet, philosopher, and devotee whose spiritual brilliance continues to illuminate generations. As the story journeys through moments of intense devotion, divine miracles, and unwavering faith, the stage transforms into a living tapestry of history and spirituality.At the heart of the narrative lies the mystical presence of Bhootaraja Rau, the fierce and devoted guardian whose loyalty and divine protection form one of the most dramatic and revered legends surrounding the saint.Blending the elegance of Bharatanatyam and Kathak, powerful music, dramatic narration, and breathtaking choreography, Svapna Vrundavana creates an immersive theatrical experience filled with grandeur, emotion, and spiritual intensity.Presented by Prabhat Arts International, this special performance is also a fundraiser supporting the Kamadhenu Project by Rotary Ulla, bringing together art, devotion, and service for a meaningful cause.Step into a world where faith becomes spectacle, legends come alive, and devotion dances across the stage.Read More

Event description: SHUFFLE DANCE WORKSHOP:Get ready to slide, hop, and groove at the Shuffle Dance Workshop, hosted by New York Dance Classes! Shuffle dance is a high-energy street style that’s all about fast footwork, rhythmic steps, and smooth transitions, often performed to electronic dance music (EDM). Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced dancer, this workshop will teach you the fundamentals of shuffling, from the iconic running man move to intricate cutting shapes.You’ll not only learn the essential techniques but also how to add your own flair and personality to every move. This workshop will help you improve your coordination, stamina, and overall flow while dancing to high-tempo beats. Perfect for anyone looking to level up their dance skills or just have a blast with EDM tunes, this class is designed to make learning fun and accessible for everyone.Meet fellow dancers, embrace the fast-paced energy, and get ready to express yourself in a supportive environment. Whether you want to shine at festivals, parties, or simply love to move to the beat, the Shuffle Dance Workshop is the perfect way to elevate your dance game!Read More

Event description: Dwayam — A Celebration of Two Voices, One SoulDwayam brings together the extraordinary musical synergy of sister performers Lakshmi Nagaraj and Indu Nagaraj, whose voices blend tradition and modernity into a single unforgettable experience. Bound by blood and united by music, the duo presents a multi-genre concert that journeys through the depths of Carnatic classical, the nostalgia and energy of filmi melodies, and the soul-stirring elegance of ghazals — creating an evening that resonates across generations.Adding to its uniqueness, Dwayam is a multilingual musical celebration, featuring songs in Kannada, Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi, reflecting the rich diversity of Indian music and connecting audiences through familiar melodies and timeless compositions.Host and Moderated by RJ Mayuurra Raghavendra, the concert goes beyond performance, offering stories, emotions, and musical conversations that reveal the artistry and bond behind the voices. Dwayam is not just a concert — it is a dialogue between two musical souls, where tradition meets contemporary expression and every note celebrates harmony, connection, and timeless artistry.Join us for an immersive musical evening on 25th April 2026 at PCPA, where melodies merge, languages unite, and two voices become one unforgettable experienceRead More
Location (Bangalore only): Prestige Centre for Performing Arts: Bengaluru

Name of the event: A Tale of Dreams and Destinies
Event description: Step into a world of wonder as The Lewis Ballet Foundation invites you to the fabled Land of Treasures, Khazaanagarh: a city where wishes change destinies, secrets underground hold treasures beyond imagination, and the world is waiting to unfold at one’s fingertips.At the heart of Khazaanagarh is Avyaan, a quick-witted street dweller whose godgifted charm keeps him ahead of trouble, often alongside his loyal friends Rafeeque and Tanishq. Beyond the clamour and crass exchanges of the bazaar lives Princess Ziva who is sheltered within palace walls, yet restless with a spirit that longs to explore the world beyond the curtains of her royal carriage. She is the daughter of a Maharaja renowned across the subcontinent as a harbinger of peace, steadfast in his rejection of corruption. Watching from the shadows is the Vizier, a man who is sharp-eyed, silver-tongued, and calculating; his ambitions reaching far beyond the throne room.When the Vizier sends Avyaan to retrieve a mysterious lamp from a hidden trove of riches, an extraordinary chain of events is set into motion. A Djinn awakens, destinies entwine, and the future of Khazaanagarh is transformed forever.Join us on this spellbinding journey where courage is tested, dreams take flight, and a single spark has the power to change everything.About The Lewis Foundation of Classical BalletEstablished in 2006, The Lewis Foundation of Classical Ballet is India’s leading institution for classical ballet training and performances. Over a thousand students learn ballet across seven centres in Bengaluru and the organisaition stages large-scale, full-length ballet productions that match global artistic and technical standards biennially.As an affiliate of the Imperial Society of Teachers of Dance (ISTD), London, and under the aegis of its Artistic Director, Ms. Yana Lewis, the foundation places Ballet, Jazz, Modern, and other allied dance disciplines at the forefront of its teaching curriculum. The organisation’s performance repertoire includes classical ballets such as Cinderella, Belle and the Beast, and Sleeping Beauty, modern ballets like Oz and Alice, curated performances like A Ballet Soirée and Mosaics of Motion, and original choreographies of Ms. Lewis herself.Emphasising the veracity of the art form, the foundation is passionately committed to bringing classical ballet to the forefront in India and become Asia’s best ballet school and company. Read More
Location (Bangalore only): Prestige Centre for Performing Arts: Bengaluru

Event description: ✨ Have you ever wondered what Lippan Art truly is?Lippan Art, also known as Mud & Mirror Work, is a traditional craft from Gujarat made using clay, mirrors, and natural materials. Its beauty comes from the harmony of earthy textures and shimmering mirror patterns that catch the light and instantly elevate any space.It is simple, grounding, meditative — and absolutely magical to create.🎨 What makes it so beautiful?Because it reflects both nature and the artist.The clay brings calmness…The mirrors bring brightness…And your hands bring life to every design.💛 What we learn while making art (as human beings):– Patience and presence– Focus and mindfulness– Confidence to express our ideas– The joy of slowing down and meeting our inner self– The freedom to create without rules– Revisiting the simple art skills we left behind after school– The happiness of making something with our own handsEvery stroke, swirl, and arrangement teaches us something about ourselves.And the final piece you create becomes a beautiful memory you can carry home and cherish forever.🖌 What you will learn in this activity:– Basics of Lippan Art– Working with clay/mud for patterns– Arranging mirrors aesthetically– Designing symmetrical or free-flowing motifs– Creating textures and clean finishes– Completing a ready-to-display art piece💫 Why choose Fabrizo?– All materials provided — just walk in and start creating– No age limit — because creativity belongs to everyone– An aesthetic, cozy ambiance that instantly relaxes you– A warm, welcoming space where you will genuinely feel at ease– The freedom to order food from anywhere while you create at your own pace– A chance to meet and connect with other fellow artists and creative people– A budget-friendly way to experience a traditional art form– Friendly guidance and a space filled with positivity🌟 Come, unwind, unleash your creativity, and discover a part of yourself you may have forgotten.At Fabrizo, Lippan Art isn’t just an activity — it’s an experience that stays with you.Disclaimer:This is a guided DIY experience, not a professional certification or training program.As we conduct multiple activities simultaneously, there may be a brief wait time. We appreciate your patience and cooperation.✨ Create. Pause. Cherish the moment.Very important Please choose to come when you do not have any other prior commitments and if you are not in a rush.Come and experience the me time or the time spent with your friends and family We sincerely request your patience and understanding while we ensure everyone receives a comfortable and enjoyable experience. Thank you for your cooperation and support. ✨Read More

Event description: One Stage. Many Icons. A True Northeast Festival in Bengaluru.Spring Fest 2026 brings an electrifying celebration of Assamese music, culture, and festival spirit to the city, headlined by a grand musical showcase celebrating Zubeen Garg, the legendary musical icon of Assam. This special edition marks the Silver Jubilee year of Spring Fest, celebrating 25 years of bringing Assamese culture to Bengaluru at scale."Symphony of Legends" MedleyA powerful celebration of Zubeen Garg’s iconic songs, uniting Achurjya Borpatra, Neel Akash, Deeplina Deka, Prabin Borah, Bornali Kalita, and Khagen Gogoi. Event description: This April, children are invited to dive into Summersault — a 15-day summer camp for children at the Prestige Centre for Performing Arts.Built around this year’s theme Namma Ooru, Bengaluru, the festival invites young participants to explore the city through theatre, movement, music, storytelling, puppetry, and visual art. Across two immersive weeks, children will discover the sounds, stories, festivals, food and personalities that make Bengaluru unique — while creating, performing and imagining together.From building city soundscapes and exploring Navarasa through theatre, to food tales, puppetry labs and heritage walks, Summersault is designed as a joyful space for curiosity, creativity and collaboration.Age Groups• 7–10 years• 11–14 yearsDates: 11th - 26th April (15 days)Timings: 9:30 AM – 1:30 PMThe festival culminates in a Final Showcase, where participants share their creations with family and friends. Come summersault into the arts this summer! Read More
Location (Bangalore only): Prestige Centre for Performing Arts: Bengaluru

Event description: 🎶 A Date with Maroon 5: Learn Ukulele Through Their Iconic Music 🎶Are you ready to learn the ukulele while diving into the world of Maroon 5? Join us for `A Date with Maroon 5,` where you’ll not only pick up the basics of playing the ukulele but also play Maroon 5`s song  "Girls like you" by the end of the session!Workshop Highlights:Warm-up & Finger Strengthening Exercises: Start your musical journey with exercises designed to strengthen your fingers and improve dexterity. These simple routines will prepare you for smooth chord transitions and better control over your strumming.Music Theory Made Simple: Understanding the basics of music theory is key to becoming a confident player. We’ll break down the essentials—notes, scales, and how they relate to your ukulele. Learn to read chord diagrams and understand why chords work together in your favorite songs.Mastering Chord Shapes: Get familiar with common ukulele chord shapes that are fundamental to Maroon 5`s music. Whether you’re a complete beginner or looking to refine your technique, we’ll ensure you get comfortable with transitioning between chords.Strumming Patterns: Discover how strumming adds rhythm and soul to your music. We`ll explore different strumming techniques, focusing on patterns that suit Maroon 5’s style, helping you bring their songs to life.Playing a Maroon 5 Song: The highlight of the workshop! After covering the basics, you’ll apply what you’ve learned by playing one of Maroon 5`s famous tracks. we’ll guide you through the chords and strumming, making it easy for you to perform the song confidently by the end of the session.What to Bring:A ukulele if you own one ( Note: we provide the uke for the duration of the workshop at no extra charges)A positive attitude and a love for Maroon 5Whether you`re a complete beginner or looking to brush up on your skills, this workshop is perfect for anyone who wants to explore the magic of music through the ukulele. By the end of the session, you’ll have the foundation needed to continue learning and playing your favorite songs.🎸 Join us for a memorable musical experience!Read More
Genre: Acoustic, Contemporary, Instrumental, International, Pop, Rock

Name of the event: Aai, Baba Aani Baayko Marathi Stand-up by Pushkar
Event description: Why should you attend?Pushkar is one of the best Marathi Standup ComedianAn extremely relatable comedy show for MaharashtriansKaaran Marathi bhasha best aahePushkar is one of the few comedians who does standup comedy shows in Hindi, English and Marathi. Name of the event: Salsa & Bachata Dance Workshop for Beginners
Event description: Join us this December at New York Dance Classes for a special Kizomba workshop (Beginners & Improvers) with the highly acclaimed Vinoth, known as Vkiz! A leading figure in the Kizomba, UrbanKiz, and Tarraxo communities, Vkiz is celebrated for his dynamic teaching methods and his ability to inspire dancers of all levels. Having conducted numerous sold-out bootcamps across cities and countries, Vkiz has been instrumental in growing the Afro-Latin dance community in India. He holds international certifications and has trained extensively in France, Scotland, and beyond, honing his expertise in Kizomba, Kizomba Fusion, Bachata, Semba, and Cuban Salsa. Known as the “silent storm” on the dance floor, Vkiz’s fluid social dancing skills and ability to connect with every dancer have earned him a strong following.This workshop is an incredible opportunity for dance enthusiasts to experience his unique teaching style, which has garnered rave reviews across global platforms. With over 10 successful festivals under his belt, Vkiz is also an organizer’s favorite. Don’t miss the chance to learn from one of the best in the industry and elevate your dance skills to a whole new level. Secure your spot on BookMyShow today and be part of this unforgettable Kizomba experience!Read More

Name of the event: Melody in Motion - An Eve of Carnatic Violin Show
Event description: Melody in Motion is an evening dedicated to the art of Carnatic violin, presented by Soundstack in association with Artkhoj. At the heart of this concert is a rare and captivating violin duet by Vidwan G Ravi Kumar, a seasoned artist from the Mysore, known for his rich tonal depth, and Vidhushi Aishwarya Lakshman, a rising talent from Bengaluru celebrated for her perfectness.  Together, they bring a flowing conversation of Swara and Laya to the stage - an interplay that is both meditative and exhilarating. Accompanying them is Vidwan Balachandra Achar on the Mridangam, whose rhythmic precision and dynamic range add a powerful foundation to the concert, and Som Sankar Naskar on percussion, whose versatile accompaniment weaves seamlessly into the Carnatic idiom. Whether you are a seasoned Rasika or someone discovering Carnatic music for the first time, Melody in Motion promises an evening that stays with you long after the last note fades.Read More

Name of the event: Own the Mic : 2 Hours to Transform Your Voice
Event description: 🗣️ What is Toastmasters?Toastmasters International is a global nonprofit organization that helps individuals become more confident communicators and leaders. With clubs in over 140 countries, Toastmasters provides a supportive environment where members can improve their public speaking, presentation, and leadership skills through practice and constructive feedback. Meeting will happen at Bhive Meeting Room - Bill Gates🎤 What Happens at a Toastmasters Event?A typical Toastmasters event is a structured meeting filled with interactive segments, including:Prepared Speeches – Members deliver planned speeches on a variety of topics.Table Topics – Impromptu speaking exercises that boost your thinking and speaking on your feet.Speech Evaluations – Constructive feedback to help speakers grow and improve.Role Plays – Members take on roles such as Timekeeper, Grammarian, and Ah-Counter to build awareness and discipline.🌟 Why Attend a Toastmasters Event?Whether you`re a seasoned speaker or just starting out, Toastmasters is a safe, encouraging space to:Build self-confidence in public speakingImprove your communication styleLearn leadership by doingNetwork with professionals from diverse backgrounds🙌 Be a Part of the ExperienceJoin us for an engaging and fun-filled session where you can observe, learn, and even participate! Everyone is welcome — you don’t need to be a member to attend.Read More

Event description: Join us for a mesmerizing evening of Indian classical music by renowned sitar maestro Ustad Shujaat Husain Khan. Khan Saab’s musical pedigree extends seven generations. He is the son and disciple of the great sitarist Ustad Vilayat Khan. His grandfather, Ustad Inayat Khan, his great-grandfather, Ustad Imdad Khan, and his great-great-grandfather, Ustad Sahebdad Khan, were all leading artists of their respective generations.Khan Saab`s musical career began at the age of three when he began practicing on a specially made small sitar. By the age of six, he was recognized as a child prodigy and began formal performances.Khan Saab has performed at all of the prestigious music festivals in India and has toured extensively through the world. Captivating audiences with his unique style of sitar play, his exceptional voice, and his intuitive and spontaneous approach to rhythm.Read More

Event description: Mondays and Wednesdays from 1st Feb 2026 to 1st May 2026This contemporary movement program focuses on building strong technical foundations while developing fluidity, control, and embodied awareness. Classes explore turns, floorwork, transitions, elevation, and dynamic movement qualities, alongside strength and conditioning.Anatomy and movement theory are integrated into practice, helping dancers understand how and why the body moves—supporting safer movement, efficiency, and intentional expression.Designed for beginners looking to start and deepen their contemporary practice, expand movement vocabulary, and build a more conscious connection to their body.About the teacher:Tanya Ballal is a performer and movement artist working across contemporary dance, pole, aerial arts, and theatre. Trained in applied and educational theatre, her practice blends physical storytelling, technique, and embodied exploration. She is the founder of Ekisa, Bangalore’s first dedicated pole and aerial movement space, and has been instrumental in building inclusive, performance-led movement communities in the city.Guidelines:Wear comfortable clothing that allows full range of motion.Classes are barefoot.Remove jewellery, watches, or accessories before class.Inform the instructor of any existing injuries, medical conditions, or movement limitations before class.This class includes floorwork, weight-bearing movement, and dynamic transitions—move at your own pace and modify as needed.Read More
Location (Bangalore only): Shoonya - Centre for Art and Somatic Practices

Name of the event: Ukulele Foundation:From Basics To Songs In 8 Weeks
Event description: Ukulele Foundation: From Basics to Songs in 8 WeeksIntroduction:Are you ready to embark on a musical journey and make your guitar dreams a reality? Whether you’re a complete beginner or have dabbled in guitar before, Ukulele Foundation is the perfect program to build your skills, confidence, and love for playing.Over 8 weeks, you’ll master the essentials of ukulele playing, from foundational techniques to strumming your favorite songs. Led by experienced instructors, this program is designed to make learning enjoyable and stress-free.What You’ll Learn:Finger Strengthening ExercisesBuild dexterity, coordination, and endurance in your fingers for effortless playing.Music Theory BasicsUnderstand the fundamental concepts of music, empowering you to read and play with confidence.Open ChordsLearn the most common chords that form the backbone of countless songs.Strumming TechniquesDevelop rhythm and style as you bring your chords to life.Song PracticePut your skills together and play popular songs that will impress friends and family.Program Details:Duration: 8 weeks (1 class per week) - 8 sessions in totalClass Length: 60 minutes per sessionWho Can Join: Beginners and early learners aged 8+Why Join This Program?Structured lessons for progressive learning.Small group sizes for personalized attention.Access to practice materials and song guides.Opportunity to showcase your progress in a final jam session.Register Now:Spaces are limited, so secure your spot today!Let the music begin! 🎸Read More
